candidate_commit_message: Refactor aoa-evals proof topology and active loop
candidate_pr_title: Refactor aoa-evals proof topology and active loop

changed_surface_groups.summary: Root design, agent guidance, root route card, and public entry routing for the bounded proof organ.
Durable decision lane for topology, validation, mechanics, reports, receipts, release readiness, and strategic closeout.
Separated direction, release narrative, quest source layout, lifecycle states, and generated quest readers.
Convex proof topology, legacy naming containment, mechanics atlas, and package route cards.
First local proof-loop smoke, bundle-local report, report index, receipt-intake dry review, release readiness, and strategic closeout reports.
Maintained Spark lane under .agents, nested AGENTS coverage, generated catalogs, runtime candidate readers, and phase-alpha matrix freshness.
Repository validation, release check, nested AGENTS validation, generated-surface checks, generated route-residue guard, and regression tests for new proof boundaries.

draft_pr_body: - add the aoa-evals root design spine, agent route mesh, proof topology map, legacy naming map, and decision lane
- restructure quest source routing into lane/state paths with lifecycle docs and generated-reader parity
- add mechanics packages for proof-object, proof-loop, release-support, audit, boundary-bridge, questbook, proof-infra, comparison-spine, publication-receipts, Titan, and Agon
- add generated route-residue validation so derived readouts cannot preserve stale active topology while part-local generated readers can keep valid sibling paths
- add generic mechanic part README validation so new parts cannot appear without routed inputs, outputs, stronger-owner split, stop-lines, and validation
- add generic mechanic provenance validation so legacy lookup routes through active-first PROVENANCE, index, distillation log, and raw-lineage boundary
- add parent-level mechanic DIRECTION surfaces so current operating direction stays separate from role cards, part maps, and provenance
- add a mechanic evidence dimension ledger so each active parent shows meaning, proof pressure, contracts, builders, quest pressure, owner split, stop-lines, and provenance
- materialize the first local proof-loop path through route-smoke, bundle-local report, report index, receipt-intake dry review, release-readiness audit, strategic closeout audit, and this PR handoff
- move the maintained Spark lane from root Spark/ into .agents/spark/ while preserving proof-boundary limits
- no tag or GitHub Release in this PR
- no bundle status promotion by changelog wording alone
- goal completion remains open until landing, GitHub Repo Validation, clean main, and final owner-visible completion audit

verification_snapshot.claim_limit: Focused handoff tests constrain this PR handoff artifact; they do not create the PR.
Focused generated-route residue tests constrain stale generated/readout references without over-policing part-local generated sibling paths.
Focused active mechanic route residue tests constrain authored route-card paths without blocking root route cards or same-part local references.
Focused mechanic payload route residue tests constrain active fixtures, schemas, manifests, scripts, tests, and hooks without blocking part-local or repo-qualified sibling refs.
Focused root authored route residue tests constrain root-facing guidance paths without blocking root route cards, historical context, or active bundle and mechanic routes.
Focused decision route residue tests constrain current-looking decision paths without erasing historical or provenance context.
Focused repo config route residue tests constrain executable config paths without erasing historical or provenance context.
Focused source bundle route residue tests constrain proof-object route paths without moving bundle meaning into mechanics.
Focused mechanic part README tests constrain generic part routing and contract headings; they do not prove any part is proof-complete.
Focused mechanic part payload inventory tests constrain part-local payload visibility and reject empty or unknown payload directories; they do not prove any part is proof-complete.
Focused mechanic part validation command tests constrain repo-relative reachable validation paths; they do not execute every part command or prove any part is proof-complete.
Focused mechanic PARTS index synchronization tests constrain local parent part maps; they do not turn cross-parent stop-line references into local ownership.
Focused mechanic legacy single-bridge tests constrain active surfaces to route archive detail through provenance; they do not prove every archived source has been distilled.
Focused mechanic provenance bridge-posture tests constrain PROVENANCE.md as a bridge, not an active route; they do not make legacy archive detail active topology.
Focused legacy naming single-bridge tests constrain docs/LEGACY_NAMING.md wording; they do not prove every archived source has been distilled.
Focused active legacy parent wording tests constrain old parent-form vocabulary in active route wording without blocking schema filename compatibility or explicit provenance context.
Focused mechanic provenance tests constrain active-first provenance routing through index, distillation log, and raw-lineage boundary; they do not make legacy active topology.
Focused mechanic parent direction tests constrain parent DIRECTION surfaces and parent entry routes; they do not create new mechanics or strengthen proof claims.
Focused mechanic evidence dimension tests constrain the parent evidence ledger; they do not make the ledger source proof authority.
Focused mechanic root-district reconnaissance tests constrain the root-district ledger; they do not move payloads or make the ledger source proof authority.
Focused root-authored surface classification tests constrain residual root docs, scripts, and tests; they do not move payloads or make root surfaces mechanic-owned.
Validates repository invariants for the current local checkout; it does not prove GitHub Repo Validation for an unmerged PR.
Checks shaped semantic AGENTS docs; it does not prove every future agent route is complete.
Checks nested route-card coverage; it does not prove source proof meaning.
Whitespace diff hygiene passed; it does not prove semantic completion.
Checks generated catalog freshness; generated output remains subordinate to source bundles.
Checks report index freshness; the report index is not receipt or verdict authority.
Checks runtime candidate template index freshness; runtime candidates remain candidate evidence.
Checks runtime candidate intake freshness; intake does not accept runtime verdict authority.
Checks phase-alpha matrix freshness; phase-alpha naming remains legacy or projection posture where applicable.
Latest-sibling canary is local compatibility evidence; it is not sibling release approval.
Full local tests passed for this checkout; tests do not prove GitHub Repo Validation or goal completion.
Local release audit passed; it is not a tag, GitHub Release, PR approval, or proof that eval claims grew stronger.

landing_steps: create a branch from current origin/main using the candidate branch name or another owner-approved branch name
commit the intended accumulated diff with the candidate commit message or an owner-approved equivalent
open a PR using the draft PR body and preserve validation, skipped checks, and boundary notes
watch GitHub Repo Validation and any required checks until the final state is green or a concrete failure is available
fix any failing check on the branch rather than weakening validation
merge only after required checks are green and owner route permits it
return to main, fast-forward from origin/main, and confirm the worktree is clean
run the final owner-visible completion audit before marking the long goal complete
claim_limit: This pre-PR handoff snapshot prepares a release-prep PR route for owner landing. At the snapshot time it did not create a branch, did not create a commit, did not push, did not open a PR, did not observe GitHub Repo Validation, did not merge, did not publish a release, did not create a tag, did not publish a GitHub Release, did not publish an eval result receipt, did not promote any bundle, did not accept runtime evidence, did not mutate sibling repos, and did not mark the goal complete. Once a branch or PR exists, current git and GitHub state supersedes this snapshot for branch, commit, push, PR, CI, and merge status.
